result.summary: HubSpot (hubapi.com) exposes multiple authenticated REST APIs under api.hubapi.com, a remote MCP server, a local developer MCP server, and two CLIs (HubSpot CLI and Agent CLI).
result.description: HubSpot is a CRM and developer platform for customer data, marketing, sales, service, CMS, and automation workflows. Its developer tooling also supports local app development and AI-agent access to HubSpot data and platform features.

hubspot_oauth_app.label: HubSpot OAuth app client credentials
hubspot_oauth_app.setup: Create an app in your HubSpot developer account using the [app creation guide](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/apps/developer-platform/build-apps/create-an-app). In the app's Auth settings, copy the `client_id` and `client_secret`, configure your `redirect_uri`, and request the scopes your integration needs. Exchange the authorization `code` for tokens as described in [Working with OAuth](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/apps/developer-platform/build-apps/authentication/oauth/working-with-oauth). Use the resulting OAuth access token as `Authorization: Bearer ...` when calling APIs.

hubspot_static_token.label: HubSpot app static auth access token
hubspot_static_token.setup: Create a privately distributed app and configure its auth type as `static` following the [authentication overview](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/apps/developer-platform/build-apps/authentication/overview) and [app creation guide](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/apps/developer-platform/build-apps/create-an-app). Copy the static auth access token from the app settings in your HubSpot account and send it as `Authorization: Bearer ...`.

hubspot_private_app_token.label: HubSpot legacy private app access token
hubspot_private_app_token.setup: In your HubSpot account, go to **Development** → **Legacy apps**, create a private app, choose scopes, then open the app's **Auth** tab and copy its access token as described in [Legacy private apps](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/apps/legacy-apps/private-apps/overview). Use it as `Authorization: Bearer ...` for HubSpot API requests.

hubspot_service_key.label: HubSpot service key
hubspot_service_key.setup: In your HubSpot account, go to **Development** → **Keys** → **Service keys**, click **Create service key**, assign scopes, then copy the key from the key details page as described in [Make API requests using a service key](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/apps/developer-platform/build-apps/authentication/account-service-keys). Use it as `Authorization: Bearer ...` for supported REST API requests and as `HUBSPOT_ACCESS_TOKEN` for admin-mode Agent CLI usage.

hubspot_dev_api_key.label: HubSpot developer API key
hubspot_dev_api_key.setup: Open the developer overview in your HubSpot account and copy your developer API key, as described in the [authentication overview](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/apps/developer-platform/build-apps/authentication/overview). Endpoints that require it document use of the `hapikey` query parameter, usually together with an `appId` query parameter.

hubspot_personal_access_key.label: HubSpot CLI personal access key
hubspot_personal_access_key.setup: Install the HubSpot CLI with [Install the HubSpot CLI](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/developer-tooling/local-development/hubspot-cli/install-the-cli), then run `hs account auth` and follow the prompts to generate a Personal Access Key in your HubSpot account and paste it into the terminal. This key is used by the HubSpot CLI's local configuration.
